title: ModelEval or ModelTrain should be called after loading the state of a PyTorch model
description: This rule checks that ModelEval or ModelTrain are called after loading the state of a PyTorch model to ensure that the model is properly initialized.
message: Call ModelEval or ModelTrain after loading the state of a PyTorch model.
impact: Performance and correctness of the model may be affected if ModelEval or ModelTrain are not called after loading the state.
recommendation: Call ModelEval or ModelTrain after loading the state of the PyTorch model.

noncompliant.code:
import torch

state_dict = torch.load('model.pt')
model = MyModel()
model.load_state_dict(state_dict)
nn = model()
noncompliant.description: ModelEval or ModelTrain is not called after loading the state.

compliant.code:
import torch

state_dict = torch.load('model.pt')
model = MyModel()
model.load_state_dict(state_dict)
model.eval()
nn = model()
compliant.description: ModelEval is called after loading the state.

compliant.code:
import torch

state_dict = torch.load('model.pt')
model = MyModel()
model.load_state_dict(state_dict)
model.train()
nn = model()
compliant.description: ModelTrain is called after loading the state.

why_is_this_an_issue: ModelEval or ModelTrain are responsible for setting the model to evaluation or training mode, which affects how the model's parameters are updated during training or how predictions are made during evaluation. Loading the state of the model without calling ModelEval or ModelTrain may result in incorrect behavior.
how_to_fix: Call ModelEval or ModelTrain after loading the state of the PyTorch model.

metadata.title: ModelEval or ModelTrain should be called after loading the state of a PyTorch model
